calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And Appointing Nominated Successor Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun, via Zoom, for and with David J. Berry, Petitioner; Jon Vaught, via Zoom, Guardian ad Litem for Osborne J. Berry.

The court confirms with Mr. Vaught that he does not object to the petition. Ms. Calhoun asks that notice to Mr. Vaught be waived. Mr. Vaught confirms in open court that he waives formal notice. The court advises Ms. Calhoun that it intends to require a bond of over $1.2 million dollars and confirms that neither she nor her client can confirm that her client is bondable for that amount. Ms. Calhoun advises that the settlor did not intend for a bond to be required. The court advises that it would require a bond regardless and points to its intentions to require a bond. Mr. Vaught advises that if the petitioner's intent is to use most of the trust assets to purchase a home, he is not too concerned with the bond issue but acknowledges the court's intentions for doing so and proposes the court order a reduced bond to cover any remaining assets, after the purchase of a home. The court confirms with the petitioner regarding the price range of the home he intends to purchase with the trust assets. After additional discussion, the court advises the parties of its tentative ruling to take the matter under submission but that it will not be allowing trustee fees. Ms. Calhoun points the court to the importance of the conditions listed within the proposed order, which are confirmed by Mr. Vaught.

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.
